The Hillyard shops Will be
thrown open again tomorrow and
about 500 employees of the Great
Northern, who have been idle Tor
nearly a week will be allowed to
go back to work.


The situation looks brighter all
the way around, according to the
statements of the officials today.
Freight is being moved over the
Northern Pacific and the Great
Northern slowly but steadily, and
passenger trains are coming back
to schedules quite rapidly.


1t is apparent, however, that
members of the Switchmen's union
in Spokane are expecting a call to
go to work on compromise, which
it is claimed the railroads are at
tempting TO make with them. It
may be three cents extra per hour
and n modification Of working ru,es
